#libs
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(vegan)
library(speedyseq)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(car)
```

#read WGS/MAGs phyloseq and PERMANOVA 1st
```{r}
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v3.rds')

bad_batch_samples <- c("B-030-2","B-027-2","B-030-1","B-027-1", "B-024-2", "B-024-1")
if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}

#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_ps_mags.rds')
#dist_method <- "euclidean"
dist_method <- "bray" # Changed variable name to avoid conflict
#ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = 'Species', NArm = TRUE)
#ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = 'Genus', NArm = TRUE)
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
#transformation <- 'clr'
transformation <- 'compositional'
sam <- data.frame(sample_data(ps), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
sam$SampleID <- rownames(sam)
sam$Strata <- NA_character_
sam$Strata[sam$Group == "A"] <- "AB"
sam$Strata[sam$Group == "C"] <- "NO"
sam$Strata[sam$Proton_Pump_Inhibitors == "Yes" & is.na(sam$Strata)] <- "PPI"
sam$Strata[sam$Group == "B" & is.na(sam$Strata) & sam$Proton_Pump_Inhibitors == "No"] <- "OTH"
sam$Strata <- factor(sam$Strata, levels = c("AB", "PPI", "OTH", "NO"))
sample_data(ps)$Strata <- sam$Strata
ps_obj <- ps
ps_obj <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, transformation)
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = det, prevalence = prev)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Corrected PERMANOVA code
metadf <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(ps_obj@sam_data))
metadf$No_Patient <- NULL
# Better way to extract metadata from phyloseq without converting to character
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Now let's fix the column types
# First, let's see what we're working with

# Convert numeric columns
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}
names(metadf)
# Convert factor columns (all the formula variables except the numeric ones we already converted)
factor_vars <- setdiff(names(metadf), numeric_cols)

for(col in factor_vars) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.factor(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Keep ID and Sample_collection_date as character (they're fine as is)
excluded_columns <- c("Batch", "No_Patient", "SampleID", "Department", "Group", "Antihypertensive_Drugs", 
                      "Anticoagulants", "Antineoplastic_Drugs", "Antilipidemic_Drugs", "Hypoglycemic_Drugs", 
                      "Asthma_Treatment_Drugs", "NSAIDs", "Antiarrhythmic_Drugs", "Antibiotics_used")
factor_vars <- setdiff(factor_vars, excluded_columns)
# Verify the conversions
#str(metadf[, c(numeric_cols, factor_vars)])

# Calculate distance matrix - FIXED: using dist_method instead of undefined 'method'
dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = dist_method)

# Define your formula variables
# Create the formula - FIXED: using dist_matrix instead of dist
formula_vars <- paste(factor_vars, collapse = " + ")
formula <- as.formula(paste("dist_matrix ~(", formula_vars, ")"))

# Run PERMANOVA - FIXED: using the correct formula and distance matrix
permanova_result <- adonis2(formula, data = metadf, by = "terms")

# Format results (your colleague's code was correct here)
res <- as.data.frame(permanova_result)
res$meta <- row.names(res)
res <- subset(res, res$meta != 'Total')
res$meta <- gsub('Residuals', 'other', res$meta)
res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` > 0.05), 'signif'] <- ' '
res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.05), 'signif'] <- '*'
res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.01), 'signif'] <- '**'
res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.001), 'signif'] <- '***'
res$Feature <- res$meta
res$Feature <- factor(res$Feature, levels = res$Feature[order(res$R2, decreasing = FALSE)])
res
```

# PERMANOVA VIF check
```{r}
# Fixed collinearity check that preserves original variable names
check_collinearity_fixed <- function(metadata, formula_vars, vif_threshold = 5) {
  # Extract the variables of interest
  vars <- unlist(strsplit(formula_vars, "\\+"))
  vars <- trimws(vars)
  
  # Subset metadata to include only relevant variables
  meta_subset <- metadata[, vars, drop = FALSE]
  
  cat("=== VARIABLE TYPES ===\n")
  print(sapply(meta_subset, class))
  
  # Convert to factors where needed
  meta_factors <- meta_subset
  for(col in names(meta_factors)) {
    if(!is.numeric(meta_factors[[col]])) {
      meta_factors[[col]] <- as.factor(meta_factors[[col]])
    }
  }
  
  # Remove constant columns
  keep_cols <- sapply(meta_factors, function(x) {
    if(is.factor(x)) return(length(levels(x)) > 1)
    return(TRUE)
  })
  meta_factors <- meta_factors[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]
  
  if(ncol(meta_factors) < 2) {
    stop("Not enough variables with variation for collinearity analysis")
  }
  
  # Calculate correlation matrix using a different approach
  # Convert all variables to numeric for correlation analysis
  meta_numeric <- meta_factors
  for(col in names(meta_numeric)) {
    if(is.factor(meta_numeric[[col]])) {
      meta_numeric[[col]] <- as.numeric(meta_numeric[[col]])
    }
  }
  
  # Remove any remaining non-numeric columns
  meta_numeric <- meta_numeric[, sapply(meta_numeric, is.numeric)]
  
  if(ncol(meta_numeric) < 2) {
    stop("Not enough numeric columns for correlation analysis")
  }
  
  # Calculate correlation matrix
  cor_matrix <- cor(meta_numeric, use = "complete.obs")
  
  cat("\n=== CORRELATION MATRIX (ORIGINAL VARIABLES) ===\n")
  print(round(cor_matrix, 3))
  
  # Find all correlation pairs
  cor_pairs <- which(upper.tri(cor_matrix), arr.ind = TRUE)
  if(length(cor_pairs) > 0) {
    all_cors <- data.frame(
      Variable1 = rownames(cor_matrix)[cor_pairs[, 1]],
      Variable2 = colnames(cor_matrix)[cor_pairs[, 2]],
      Correlation = round(cor_matrix[cor_pairs], 3)
    )
    all_cors$Abs_Correlation <- abs(all_cors$Correlation)
    all_cors <- all_cors[order(-all_cors$Abs_Correlation), ]
    
    cat("\n=== CORRELATION PAIRS (sorted by |r|) ===\n")
    print(all_cors)
  }
  
  # Calculate VIF using the original variable names
  calculate_vif_original <- function(data) {
    # Remove rows with missing values for VIF calculation
    data_complete <- data[complete.cases(data), , drop = FALSE]
    if(nrow(data_complete) < 2) return(NULL)
    
    # Create a dummy response variable
    set.seed(123)
    dummy_response <- rnorm(nrow(data_complete))
    
    # Build formula with original variables
    predictor_vars <- colnames(data_complete)
    formula_str <- paste("dummy_response ~", paste(predictor_vars, collapse = " + "))
    
    # Fit model and calculate VIF
    lm_model <- lm(as.formula(formula_str), data = data_complete)
    
    # Use tryCatch for VIF calculation
    vif_values <- tryCatch({
      car::vif(lm_model)
    }, error = function(e) {
      # If VIF fails, use manual calculation
      cat("VIF calculation warning:", e$message, "\n")
      cat("Using manual VIF calculation...\n")
      
      # Manual VIF calculation: VIF = 1 / (1 - R^2)
      vif_manual <- numeric(length(predictor_vars))
      names(vif_manual) <- predictor_vars
      
      for(i in seq_along(predictor_vars)) {
        current_var <- predictor_vars[i]
        other_vars <- predictor_vars[-i]
        
        if(length(other_vars) > 0) {
          formula_other <- paste(current_var, "~", paste(other_vars, collapse = " + "))
          lm_other <- lm(as.formula(formula_other), data = data_complete)
          r2 <- summary(lm_other)$r.squared
          vif_manual[i] <- 1 / (1 - r2)
        } else {
          vif_manual[i] <- 1  # No other variables
        }
      }
      return(vif_manual)
    })
    
    return(vif_values)
  }
  
  # Calculate VIF
  vif_values <- calculate_vif_original(meta_numeric)
  
  if(!is.null(vif_values)) {
    cat("\n=== VIF VALUES (ORIGINAL VARIABLES) ===\n")
    print(vif_values)
    
    # Identify high VIF variables
    high_vif <- names(vif_values)[vif_values > vif_threshold]
    
    if(length(high_vif) > 0) {
      cat("\nVariables with high VIF (>", vif_threshold, "):", paste(high_vif, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    } else {
      cat("\nNo variables with high VIF found\n")
    }
    
    return(list(
      vif_values = vif_values,
      high_vif_vars = high_vif,
      cor_matrix = cor_matrix,
      all_correlations = if(exists("all_cors")) all_cors else NULL,
      remaining_vars = setdiff(vars, high_vif)
    ))
  } else {
    cat("Could not calculate VIF values\n")
    return(list(
      vif_values = NULL,
      high_vif_vars = NULL,
      cor_matrix = cor_matrix,
      all_correlations = if(exists("all_cors")) all_cors else NULL,
      remaining_vars = vars
    ))
  }
}
```

#refine PERMANOVA model pt.1
```{r}
refine_model_stepwise_one_at_a_time <- function(ps_obj, dist_method, metadata, current_vars, alpha = 0.05, max_iterations = 20) {
  cat("=== STEPWISE REFINEMENT (ONE VARIABLE AT A TIME) ===\n")
  
  best_model <- NULL
  best_vars <- current_vars
  best_r2 <- 0
  iteration <- 1
  
  while(iteration <= max_iterations && length(current_vars) > 1) {
    # Run PERMANOVA with current variables
    dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = dist_method)
    formula <- as.formula(paste("dist_matrix ~(", paste(current_vars, collapse = "+"),")"))
    current_model <- adonis2(formula, data = metadata, by = "terms")
    
    # Extract p-values and calculate total R2
    p_values <- current_model$`Pr(>F)`[1:length(current_vars)]
    names(p_values) <- current_vars
    total_r2 <- sum(current_model$R2[1:length(current_vars)], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Check for significant variables
    significant_vars <- names(p_values)[p_values < alpha & !is.na(p_values)]
    
    cat(sprintf("\nIteration %d: %d variables, R² = %.3f, %d significant\n",
                iteration, length(current_vars), total_r2, length(significant_vars)))
    cat("Variables:", paste(current_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    cat("P-values:", paste(round(p_values, 3), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    
    # Track best model (prioritizing models with significant variables)
    if(length(significant_vars) > 0 && total_r2 > best_r2) {
      best_model <- current_model
      best_vars <- current_vars
      best_r2 <- total_r2
      cat("✓ New best model found!\n")
    }
    
    # Stop if we have significant variables
    if(length(significant_vars) > 0) {
      cat("✓ Model contains significant variables - stopping refinement\n")
      break
    }
    
    # Remove ONLY the variable with the highest p-value
    if(length(p_values) > 1) {
      highest_p_var <- names(which.max(p_values))
      current_vars <- setdiff(current_vars, highest_p_var)
      cat("→ Removing", highest_p_var, "(p =", round(max(p_values, na.rm = TRUE), 3), ")\n")
      cat("Remaining variables:", paste(current_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    } else {
      break
    }
    
    iteration <- iteration + 1
  }
  
  # If we never found a model with significant variables, use the last model
  if(is.null(best_model) && length(current_vars) > 0) {
    dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = dist_method)
    formula <- as.formula(paste("dist_matrix ~(", paste(current_vars, collapse = "+"),")"))
    best_model <- adonis2(formula, data = metadata, by = "terms")
    best_vars <- current_vars
  }
  
  return(list(permanova_result = best_model, used_vars = best_vars))
}

# Updated corrected workflow
corrected_workflow_one_at_a_time <- function(ps_obj, dist_method, metadata, formula_vars, vif_threshold = 5, alpha = 0.05) {
  
  # Step 1: Remove variables with high VIF (ONE TIME ONLY)
  collinearity_results <- check_collinearity_fixed(metadata, formula_vars, vif_threshold)
  remaining_after_vif <- collinearity_results$remaining_vars
  
  cat("=== AFTER VIF REMOVAL ===\n")
  cat("Remaining variables:", paste(remaining_after_vif, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("Removed due to VIF >", vif_threshold, ":", paste(collinearity_results$high_vif_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Step 2: Perform stepwise refinement (one variable at a time) on VIF-corrected variables
  refined_results <- refine_model_stepwise_one_at_a_time(
    ps_obj = ps_obj,
    dist_method = dist_method,
    metadata = metadata,
    current_vars = remaining_after_vif,
    alpha = alpha
  )
  
  return(list(
    permanova_result = refined_results$permanova_result,
    used_vars = refined_results$used_vars,
    vif_removed_vars = collinearity_results$high_vif_vars,
    collinearity_info = collinearity_results
  ))
}
```

# 1st refinement PERMANOVA model launch
```{r}
# Run with one-at-a-time removal
final_results <- corrected_workflow_one_at_a_time(
  ps_obj = ps_obj,
  dist_method = dist_method,
  metadata = metadf,
  formula_vars = formula_vars,
  vif_threshold = 5,
  alpha = 0.05
)

# Print final results
cat("\n=== FINAL RESULTS ===\n")
cat("Removed due to VIF:", paste(final_results$vif_removed_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Final variables:", paste(final_results$used_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
print(final_results$permanova_result)
current_model_result <- final_results
```

# further improvements
```{r}
# Function to further refine model by removing insignificant variables while keeping significant ones
refine_model_keep_significant <- function(ps_obj, dist_method, metadata, current_model_result, alpha = 0.05) {
  
  # Extract current variables and results
  current_vars <- current_model_result$used_vars
  current_permanova <- current_model_result$permanova_result
  
  cat("=== FURTHER REFINEMENT (KEEPING SIGNIFICANT VARIABLES) ===\n")
  cat("Starting with variables:", paste(current_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Extract p-values from current model
  model_df <- as.data.frame(current_permanova)
  p_values <- model_df$`Pr(>F)`[1:length(current_vars)]
  names(p_values) <- current_vars
  
  # Identify significant variables
  significant_vars <- names(p_values)[p_values < alpha & !is.na(p_values)]
  insignificant_vars <- names(p_values)[p_values >= alpha | is.na(p_values)]
  
  cat("Significant variables:", paste(significant_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("Insignificant variables:", paste(insignificant_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # If no significant variables, return original model
  if(length(significant_vars) == 0) {
    cat("No significant variables found - returning original model\n")
    return(current_model_result)
  }
  
  # Start with just the significant variables
  refined_vars <- significant_vars
  best_model <- NULL
  best_vars <- refined_vars
  best_r2 <- 0
  
  cat("\nStarting refinement with significant variables only...\n")
  
  # Try adding back insignificant variables one by one, if they improve the model
  for(var in insignificant_vars) {
    test_vars <- c(refined_vars, var)
    
    # Test if adding this variable improves the model
    dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = dist_method)
    formula <- as.formula(paste("dist_matrix ~(", paste(current_vars, collapse = "+"),")"))
    test_model <- adonis2(formula, data = metadata, by = "terms")
    
    # Extract p-value for the added variable
    test_p_values <- test_model$`Pr(>F)`[1:length(test_vars)]
    names(test_p_values) <- test_vars
    added_var_p <- test_p_values[var]
    
    # Calculate total R2
    test_r2 <- sum(test_model$R2[1:length(test_vars)], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Check if the added variable is significant or doesn't harm the model too much
    if(added_var_p < alpha * 2) {  # Liberal threshold for keeping variables
      refined_vars <- c(refined_vars, var)
      best_model <- test_model
      best_vars <- refined_vars
      best_r2 <- test_r2
      cat("+ Keeping", var, "(p =", round(added_var_p, 3), ", R² =", round(test_r2, 3), ")\n")
    } else {
      cat("- Discarding", var, "(p =", round(added_var_p, 3), ")\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Final model with refined variables
  if(is.null(best_model)) {
    dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = dist_method)
    formula <- as.formula(paste("dist_matrix ~", paste(refined_vars, collapse = "+")))
    best_model <- adonis2(formula, data = metadata, by = "terms")
    best_vars <- refined_vars
  }
  
  return(list(
    permanova_result = best_model,
    used_vars = best_vars,
    original_vars = current_vars,
    significant_vars_kept = significant_vars,
    insignificant_vars_removed = setdiff(current_vars, best_vars)
  ))
}
```

#aggressive removal of insignificant variables
```{r}
# More aggressive approach - remove all insignificant variables at once
refine_model_aggressive <- function(ps_obj, dist_method, metadata, current_model_result, alpha = 0.05) {
  
  current_vars <- current_model_result$used_vars
  current_permanova <- current_model_result$permanova_result
  
  cat("=== AGGRESSIVE REFINEMENT ===\n")
  cat("Starting variables:", paste(current_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Extract p-values
  model_df <- as.data.frame(current_permanova)
  p_values <- model_df$`Pr(>F)`[1:length(current_vars)]
  names(p_values) <- current_vars
  
  # Keep only significant variables
  significant_vars <- names(p_values)[p_values < alpha & !is.na(p_values)]
  
  cat("Keeping only significant variables:", paste(significant_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # If no significant variables, keep the 2-3 most significant ones
  if(length(significant_vars) == 0) {
    cat("No significant variables at alpha =", alpha, "- keeping most promising variables\n")
    # Sort by p-value and keep the top 2-3
    sorted_vars <- names(sort(p_values))[1:min(3, length(p_values))]
    significant_vars <- sorted_vars
    cat("Keeping:", paste(significant_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  }
  
  # Fit final model
  dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = dist_method)
  formula <- as.formula(paste("dist_matrix ~", paste(significant_vars, collapse = "+")))
  final_model <- adonis2(formula, data = metadata, by = "terms")
  
  return(list(
    permanova_result = final_model,
    used_vars = significant_vars,
    original_vars = current_vars,
    removed_vars = setdiff(current_vars, significant_vars)
  ))
}
```

# run model improvement
```{r}
# Assuming you have your current results from the previous step
# current_results <- corrected_workflow_one_at_a_time(...)

# Option 1: Conservative refinement (tries to keep variables that help)
refined_results <- refine_model_keep_significant(
  ps_obj = ps_obj,
  dist_method = dist_method,
  metadata = metadf,
  current_model_result = current_model_result,  # Your current results object
  alpha = 0.05
)

# Option 2: Aggressive refinement (removes all insignificant)
aggressive_results <- refine_model_aggressive(
  ps_obj = ps_obj,
  dist_method = dist_method,
  metadata = metadf,
  current_model_result = current_model_result,
  alpha = 0.05
)

# Compare the results
cat("\n=== COMPARISON ===\n")
cat("Original variables:", paste(current_model_result$used_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Conservative refinement:", paste(refined_results$used_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Aggressive refinement:", paste(aggressive_results$used_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Print the refined models
cat("\n=== CONSERVATIVE MODEL ===\n")
print(refined_results$permanova_result)

cat("\n=== AGGRESSIVE MODEL ===\n")
print(aggressive_results$permanova_result)
```



# stepwise advancement of the last model
```{r}
# Continue stepwise refinement from current model
continue_stepwise_refinement <- function(ps_obj, dist_method, metadata, current_model_result, alpha = 0.05) {
  
  current_vars <- current_model_result$used_vars
  cat("=== CONTINUING STEPWISE REFINEMENT ===\n")
  cat("Current variables:", paste(current_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Use the same one-at-a-time function but start from current variables
  refined_results <- refine_model_stepwise_one_at_a_time(
    ps_obj = ps_obj,
    dist_method = dist_method,
    metadata = metadata,
    current_vars = current_vars,
    alpha = alpha
  )
  
  return(list(
    permanova_result = refined_results$permanova_result,
    used_vars = refined_results$used_vars,
    original_vars = current_vars
  ))
}

# Use this to continue refinement
continued_results <- continue_stepwise_refinement(
  ps_obj = ps_obj,
  dist_method = dist_method,
  metadata = metadf,
  current_model_result = current_model_result,
  alpha = 0.05
)
```

#complete workflow
```{r}
# Step 1: Clone your current results
current_results <- final_results

# Step 2: Print what we're starting with
cat("=== STARTING POINT FOR FURTHER REFINEMENT ===\n")
cat("Current variables:", paste(current_results$used_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Variables removed due to VIF:", paste(current_results$vif_removed_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Step 3: Try different refinement approaches
# Option A: Conservative refinement (recommended first)
conservative_results <- refine_model_keep_significant(
  ps_obj = ps_obj,
  dist_method = dist_method,
  metadata = metadf,
  current_model_result = current_results,
  alpha = 0.05
)

# Option B: Aggressive refinement
aggressive_results <- refine_model_aggressive(
  ps_obj = ps_obj,
  dist_method = dist_method,
  metadata = metadf,
  current_model_result = current_results,
  alpha = 0.05
)

# Option C: Continue stepwise refinement
continued_results <- continue_stepwise_refinement(
  ps_obj = ps_obj,
  dist_method = dist_method,
  metadata = metadf,
  current_model_result = current_results,
  alpha = 0.05
)

# Step 4: Compare all approaches
cat("\n=== COMPARISON OF ALL APPROACHES ===\n")
approaches <- list(
  "Current" = current_results,
  "Conservative" = conservative_results,
  "Aggressive" = aggressive_results,
  "Continued" = continued_results
)

comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Approach = names(approaches),
  Variables = sapply(approaches, function(x) paste(x$used_vars, collapse = ", ")),
  Num_Variables = sapply(approaches, function(x) length(x$used_vars)),
  Total_R2 = sapply(approaches, function(x) {
    sum(x$permanova_result$R2[1:length(x$used_vars)], na.rm = TRUE)
  }),
  Significant_Vars = sapply(approaches, function(x) {
    p_vals <- x$permanova_result$`Pr(>F)`[1:length(x$used_vars)]
    sum(p_vals < 0.05 & !is.na(p_vals))
  })
)

print(comparison_table)

# Step 5: Detailed analysis of each approach
for(approach_name in names(approaches)) {
  approach <- approaches[[approach_name]]
  cat(sprintf("\n=== %s APPROACH ===\n", toupper(approach_name)))
  cat("Variables:", paste(approach$used_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  print(approach$permanova_result)
}
```
